mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment indicates labor market slack, reducing consumer spending power and corporate earnings, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.0, well above 5% natural rate
failure_condition: If UNRATE rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window, or if a decrease in UNRATE is not followed by positive 20-day returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above the zero-lower-bound threshold signals the start of a tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative short-term forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.07, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time after FEDFUNDS>0.5% triggers, or if the signal has no significant rank IC (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in retail sales and payroll employment signal robust consumer demand and income growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive 20-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:both series historically trend together during expansions
failure_condition: If the joint signal of both series increasing does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) exceeds the 100 baseline index, the economy is in an industrial expansion regime where supply-side strength drives GDP growth and equity appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=94.1851 currently below 100, not in expansion
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO>100 (one-sided t-test p>0.05), or if the frequency of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 700 thousand units (seasonally adjusted annual rate) reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, boosting economic growth and leading to positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in less than 50% of instances when HOUST>700, or if the threshold-triggered signal has no predictive power (rank IC<0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the CPI (above 0.3% month-over-month) signals accelerating inflation that erodes real earnings and may force the Fed to tighten, leading to negative 5-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI level=226.955; trend unknown but threshold defined
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after CPI spikes are positive more often than negative (50% threshold), or if the signal's rank IC is not negative as predicted.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive spread between the unemployment rate and the federal funds rate indicates highly accommodative monetary policy, which supports risk assets and drives positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: If the spread UNRATE - FEDFUNDS does not show a significant positive rank IC (p<0.10) in walk-forward cross-section, or if positive returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of payroll employment increases (rising in at least 2 of the last 3 months) signals an expanding labor market that boosts aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > previous month value in at least 2 of last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131516; need prior months to verify trend
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of instances when the employment expansion condition is met, or if the rolling rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year period.
